text: Large language models, or LLMs, are a type of artificial intelligence that can understand and generate human-like text. They work by learning patterns from vast amounts of text data, such as books, websites, and articles. This training data helps them predict the next word in a sentence, enabling them to write essays, answer questions, or hold conversations. To get good results, people use prompt engineering—carefully crafting questions or instructions to guide the model. LLMs have impressive capabilities, like translating languages or summarizing information. However, they have limits: they can produce incorrect facts, lack true understanding, and rely heavily on their training data, which may contain biases.
